text.title: SDD Workflow Overview Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding SDD Workflow Overview. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Phases execute in fixed order spec → plan → tasks → readiness → done; skipping requires a documented override.
rule.rationale: Five-phase lifecycle; `plan` merges old design+impl-plan into one plan.md (see plan-md-structure); `readiness` is the new gate before `done` (see readiness-checklist).

rule.statement: Every phase emits a versioned artefact with semver version + last_reviewed date.
rule.rationale: Every phase emits a versioned artefact with semver version + last_reviewed date.

rule.statement: Promotion to the next phase requires a documented gate result from the previous phase.
rule.rationale: Promotion to the next phase requires a documented gate result from the previous phase.

rule.statement: The SDD manifest is the single source of truth for current phase; status in code or chat does not count.
rule.rationale: The SDD manifest is the single source of truth for current phase; status in code or chat does not count.

rule.statement: Code work cannot start without a spec artefact at minimum; design/impl-plan can be lightweight but spec is mandatory.
rule.rationale: Code work cannot start without a spec artefact at minimum; design/impl-plan can be lightweight but spec is mandatory.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-sdd-workflow-overview.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
